description: Lock the chronology and direct relationship semantics for courier-carried medieval merchant market reports.

checks.reason: A 29 November 1265 Sienese merchant letter reports the current Troyes florin exchange rate and compares it with the preceding Provins fair.
The scoped reports carried information generated by recurring Champagne fair markets.
Fairs generated the changing exchange conditions that the correspondence reported.
Fair couriers transported the reports between Champagne and Italian merchant houses.
Courier transport is constitutive of the narrowed circulated-report scope.
Paper lowered letter-production costs while parchment remained an alternative.
